verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of immunoglobulin-like domain proteins. The founding member IZUMO1 is a single-pass TM protein critical for sperm-egg fusion, surface-displayed on the outer leaflet of sperm. IZUMO4 shares the Izumo domain but has been reported as a GPI-anchored or secreted/peripheral member with expression highly restricted to germline/reproductive lineages (testis-enriched). If GPI-anchored or TM-containing, it would reach the outer leaflet only in sperm or germ cells — a narrow germline/tissue-restricted compartment — qualifying as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface. The biology is not well characterized enough to confirm stable broad surface presence, and expression outside germline tissue is not established. No clinical surface-directed therapeutic programs are documented. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer ecto-pool; no cell-death surface display; no lysosomal exocytosis story; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering outside germline; narrow germline lineage restriction confirmed.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4's precise membrane topology (GPI-anchored vs. secreted vs. TM) and whether it truly reaches the outer leaflet in any cell type is poorly characterized; family-lineage inference from IZUMO1 may not hold.
